name: Khu căn cứ Vàm Sát Đảo Khỉ

reviews.author: Thap4Aca Ngo
reviews.text: Nice place with a good 1.5 km hike, surrounded by lots of monkeys and an alligator pit. The site also offers a boat tour, which cost 800,000 VND for the entire boat (round trip). I took my five-year-old, and he absolutely loved it. It was a fun and educational experience, especially with the Vietnam War history shared throughout the area. I recommend bringing a hat and plenty of sunscreen, and make sure to watch your belongings—the monkeys here are known to steal glasses and anything else they can grab. Overall, a pretty cool experience and well worth the visit.

reviews.author: Cliff G
reviews.text: This place did not disappoint. You must listen to the guides. These monkeys will scheme and plot on you. Keep your glasses in your pocket or bag. Don't leave your bag alone. The monkey's will try to steal everything you have.

reviews.author: Nguyen Huong Giang
reviews.text: Cool place to learn about monkeys. A nice walk into the jungle. Ideally spending 1 hour here. Be careful with the mosquitoes coming out at sunset. Entrance fee is 35k/person.

reviews.author: Eva Mallene
reviews.text: while visiting you have two options: skip the walk through the forest of monkeys and take a speed canoe ride to the end of the park for an option to wonder around the Sac Forest War Base and then take the boat back, or take a walk. the boat costs 800k for up to 6 people (fixed price for round-trip, one boatload). we took the boat but then decided to walk as well and immediately a monkey climbed up on my head and tried to steal my glasses :) so if you need glasses to see, i suggest you just take the boat.

reviews.author: C Nick’s
reviews.text: Solo traveler. Made my way with public bus follow by ferry and another bus. Took about 2.5hrs to reach the location which isn't too difficult to find. At the entrance, the park ranger will remind you not to have food, drinks, caps, glasses, mobile phones and etc exposed or you risk having your items snatched by the monkeys. I have read the same from other forums so I was extremely careful when making my way in. I didn't go for the boat trip. I walked and its all worth it. Once you get passed the monkey's territory, you'll be welcome by this beautiful mangrove canopy which stretches all the way to the Rung Sac base. There are public toilets along the way. Immerse yourself in the environment while watching a video about the war and the base. In my opinion, way better than War Remnant Museum. On my way out, I got complacent as I couldn't see clearly without my glasses. So I put them on and kept a look out for the monkeys and kept my hand on my glasses. Nope it didn't help. One monkey jumped onto my back and snatched my glasses away. Luckily the park ranger helped me get it back. Very soon after, another tried to grab my hat but I got it well secured. Besides the monkey business in this park, the trip was probably one of my best on my itinerary to Ho Chih Min city.

reviews.author: L Santiago
reviews.text: the mangrove forest is beautiful. walking through the forest is also very easy as the terrain was mostly flat. monkeys were chill during my visit - it was so nice to see them swinging and walking around. they seem to have easy access to trash though so they scatter litter when they scavenge for food. hopefully trash can be secured better.

reviews.author: Fer Lopez
reviews.text: We visited this place on a weekday. Good for a walk in nature. The museum is nice, with a lot of history, only one location to see monkeys. The fee for foreigners is higher than locals. The ride in the boat is also a bit pricy. We walked around for 2 hours, inside nature and mangroves. Some monkeys seem sick, and some trash around that, but it can be fixed easy. It is around 2 hours from Ho Chi Minh City, Including a ferry.

reviews.author: DHARANI KRISHNAA
reviews.text: Beaware of those monkeys they are a bit playful and steal your stuff like scarf, any food, phones and hats.

reviews.author: Scott Witkowsky
reviews.text: This was a memorable place to visit while in Vietnam. The monkeys are everywhere (except the crocodile pen). The monkeys here reeeeeaaaallly like ice cream. If you walk anywhere with some, they’re going to snatch it. Rumor has it that ice cream can be traded to get back your sunglasses (which they love), hats (which they love), phones (which they love), or any other physical object (which YOU love). Leave as much as you can in the car, bus, or people movers. Rangers can often get stuff back for you, but sometimes they’re just not there when it happens. Some tour guides liked to carry sticks to fend off the monkeys, but aided by our tour guide, we were wisely told that if a monkey does approach you or jump on you, try not to freak out. They are most interested in what they can steal off of you (don’t have it on you in the first place) and they just want to smell you and see what you’re all about.

reviews.author: Amy H
reviews.text: If you can ask for the guards to take you in to feed them. It’s a fun experience because they know the monkeys and which ones are mean. They also protect you from the monkeys stealing your stuff. Super fun :)

reviews.author: Amy Hynd
reviews.text: The monkeys are adorable. Please listen to direction. I observed another tourist who decided to bring food with them get angry when the monkeys stole a bag of chips. I wish the park staff had done something when he started swinging a stick at the poor little monkeys

reviews.author: Dominic Kirby
reviews.text: BRITISH SCOOTER DAY TRIP VISIT - We wanted to do a day trip from the city on our scooter. This park is 221,000d when accounting for the extra fees (for two people). We were so worried about being attacked, we planned accordingly. Long trousers and no bling. We were very cautious and had no issues with the monkeys. We did however see other guests be harassed. Bring absolutely nothing and you will be ok! The park was very empty and run down, we only stayed for around 20 mins. That being said, when accounting for the scooter ferry and long ride through the countryside it was well worth the day trip! Would recommend.

reviews.author: Vincent Oliver
reviews.text: The name is misleading as the Rung Sac War Zone Historic Site, amidst the amazing mangrove swamps, was the main attraction. It’s a 1km walk into the swamps, a lot of wooden boardwalk above the mud. Well worth a visit! The many monkeys at the entrance did not bother us at all, like previous reviews said ( but we took no food, glasses etc). There are motorboat ride at extra cost (d600k/boat) but depends on the tidal level of the water in the swamps. The museum was closed but lunch in the restaurant was tasty and average price. Park entry is d70k foreigner d35k Vietnamese. An optional d10k return ride on a electric cart, or a short 1km? walk from the parking area.

reviews.author: Valentin LABOURDETTE
reviews.text: Funny experience. There really is a LOT of monkeys. It can actually be quite impressive or even scary. Don’t give them food if you don’t want them to climb on you. There is also a crocodile farm where you can feed them ! Very impressive !

reviews.author: LINH
reviews.text: Good for people who want to explore local history and nature. About 1 hour by driving from Ho Chi Minh city. The weather is extremely hot, thus recommend to using electric car to commute from main gate to visiting area (about 1km). You van buy tickets at the gate (reservation is not necessary). You should bring a lot a water and personal fan. Be careful with your snack and belongings since the monkeys there is quite aggressive.

reviews.author: Ksenia Travels
reviews.text: Great place for a day trip away from busy Ho Chi Minh City! Enjoy a wonderful stroll along the mangroves, see lots of macaques and crocodiles. And a visit to the American-Vietnamese war Base in the same park! Bring lots of water and be careful with monkeys as they get aggressive sometimes. And PLEASE DO NOT THROW ANY TRASH IN THE PARK, monkeys eat it and suffer! Keep the park clean!!!

reviews.author: David Vedder
reviews.text: Very beautiful UNESCO listed Mangrove Forest. It takes about 2-3 hours to walk around the place, and the forest is very pretty, and you can meet and take photos of a bunch of wild monkies. There are 2 populations of wild monkies, be careful of the brown/longtailed ones, they will steal your stuff, especially glasses, and ransom them for food. Do not eat or drink in front of them. The yellow/short tailed ones are much more well behaved. The facilities are a bit rudimentary, but are functional. Some of the bridges and walkways are rickety, so watch your footing/weight distribution. There is a replica NVA Guerilla camp with raised walkways which have been added for your convenience. The camp gives some history to the fighters that lived in the area, and an idea of their living conditions. Overall, I think it's worthwhile to visit.

reviews.author: 신지웅
reviews.text: It's fun You can see monkeys' bandits in front of you and humiliate a three-meter-tall crocodile! Overall, it was fun, and I liked the tour schedule quite a bit. Our guide was Sean from SST Travel He was a guide who enjoyed it more than us :) It's great

reviews.author: Igor Bajić
reviews.text: a place where you can spend an exciting time watching monkeys and feeding them, you can also see crocodiles...there is also a restaurant but it was not open during my visit...👌P.S. keep your jewelry, cell phone, glasses, monkeys will steal it and later exchange it for some flips, or food...😉😄

reviews.author: Loi Cao
reviews.text: This place is pretty much fine, yes the "guards" don't really protect anyone, although the one they should protect would be the animals, you can finely walk through the route without attracting the monkeys with your food, or anything else you keep in your hand, just keep it in your bag and closed have a sip of water when there are less or none around, they also steal glasses quite often, i understand if u have prescription glasses that u might not be able to see much, so if you decide to keep them on don't let the monkeys get to the point where they are able to snatch them away, know to evade, but please don't attack the monkeys, seriously can't you even not eat food within 1-2 hour walk through a forest, it is even written on the board to not bring any food with you, the monkeys do follow you, but in my case, as long as i didn't attract their attention unnecessarily they wouldn't do nothing, i saw some visitors carrying a stick to beat the monkeys, such people should be heavily fined, behave yourself, you're in foreign environment, a one that we stole and have deprived of resources, so have some respect or don't even bother to visit, you can bring the monkeys some natural food, they do fight each other if it's disposed irresponsibly.

reviews.author: Kha Nguyen
reviews.text: I love the dark feeling it brings to us during the sunset time. A little mysterious and scary, but interesting. It's a great place for filming and taking photos!

reviews.author: Christian Diaz
reviews.text: Monkey island delivered, it was a bit of long but a fun ride around 4 hours, considering both ways form HCM city. But totally worth it, 100 k per entrance , loads of monkey (some not friendly) Crocs and vietnamise war exhibits. Really cool , just BEWARE OF THE CRIP MONKEYS ! THEY WILD , no water , no food , no glasses basically don't bring nothing , they will jack you and attack you as well. BE WARNED!

reviews.author: Noah Miller
reviews.text: The place was not well maintained and nothing was really explained in English. Without a guide an English speaker will probably feel a bit lost. If the weather had been better coastal seafood would've been on the agenda afterwards. As it was during the rainy season it is not a great place to go.

reviews.author: Baf Weekend
reviews.text: I believed in spend an hour to relax and commune with nature. Some unexpected things from monkeys but its ok. Beautiful place for selfies especially for the nature lovers

reviews.author: Aryan Khalili
reviews.text: Very unique and amazing experience. 2 hours drive from HCMC. Staff tour were Nice and positive . Bus 🚌 has its own WiFi. Food during the lunch was delicious. My tour did include swimming but I was never told about it. Monkeys 🐒 were very playful. They steal your things so watch out. Good place to take photos.

reviews.author: Diego Arana
reviews.text: This is an interesting spot if you are a local. If you are a tourist and you are going by scooter, it is a waste of time. Monkey Island is not really interesting. Monkeys are rude. You can take a nice picture if the guides give you the chance to feed them. I put 3 stars because the way is amazing (mangroves and fresh seafood market).

reviews.author: Damien Dykstra
reviews.text: Great experience and definitely a place to remember. Didn’t expect it to be a base camp for the Vietnamese during the war, so that was an awesome bonus as well. Make sure to not have anything out or take photos quickly and carefully😁

reviews.author: Johnny Marbles
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful place, a green lung of Saigon. Very close to the beach (though the beach is not very clean and beautiful like in Vung Tau or Nha Trang.) This is a mangrove forest for storm preventing as well as flood preventing. A natural-based system that is more effective than human-built one.

reviews.author: Hien Nguyen
reviews.text: Monkey Island in Can Gio is one of the most unique attractions near Ho Chi Minh City, offering visitors a chance to experience both nature and wildlife. Located about 50 kilometers from the city center, this eco-tourism site is famous for its large population of wild monkeys that roam freely around the area. For many travelers, it is both exciting and amusing to see the monkeys up close, though you need to be careful with your belongings as they are quite mischievous. Beyond the monkeys, the island is part of the Can Gio Mangrove Biosphere Reserve, recognized by UNESCO, which provides a diverse natural environment of mangroves, rivers, and rich birdlife. Visitors can take boat trips through the mangrove forest, explore wartime relics, and learn more about the importance of this ecosystem to southern Vietnam. While facilities are basic, the charm of Monkey Island lies in its wild, natural setting and the memorable encounters with the monkeys. It’s a refreshing weekend getaway from the busy city, perfect for families and nature lovers alike.

reviews.author: Tam Phan
reviews.text: This place is wonderful and gives you an up close look at the monkeys there. Just a caution though is that I highly recommend that you keep loose items in backpacks or in your vehicles because they will take anything they can possible get a hold of. Other than that I had a wonderful time here and the staff are very polite.
